content.title: Δ*fur* derepression drives the lipid A-loss-permissive envelope state in *Caulobacter crescentus*
content.affiliation: U.C. Berkeley / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
content.subtitle: Δ*fur* derepression is the demonstrable driver of the lipid A-loss-permissive envelope state, and everything downstream is the supporting (and still-open) mechanism it sets in motion.

speaker_notes_seed: Open by grounding the audience: Caulobacter crescentus is a freshwater α-proteobacterium with an unusual capacity — documented by Zik 2022 — to survive and grow after losing lipid A from its outer membrane, provided Fur is absent. This talk asks the mechanistic question: what regulatory and proteomic architecture makes that loss permissive? The one-sentence answer is the subtitle — Δfur derepression is the demonstrable driver, and the five downstream layers (Leaden concordance, Path A/B fitness triage, ChvI two-phase response, sphingolipid/Lpt remodeling, PG reorganization) each contribute one supporting or still-open layer of mechanism. Name the STRONG-tier evidence standard up front if this is a specialist audience: all findings are graded by a pre-registered scorecard; negatives (CtpA rejected, SigU not supported) are reported honestly alongside the supported claims.

evidence_anchors.ref: REPORT.md title — 'The regulatory and proteomic architecture of Δfur-permitted lipid A loss in Caulobacter crescentus'

revision_log.summary: Replaced garbled auto-title 'Caulobacter Fur Lipida Loss' (non-word 'Lipida'; wrong subject noun) with REPORT-grounded claim title matching the throughline; populated presenter (Adam Arkin) and affiliation (U.C. Berkeley / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory) in place of TBD placeholders; completed the truncated subtitle to the full verbatim throughline punchline sentence, removing the dangling 'and everything…' fragment.
